mnemonics: S.P.U.B. (Select, Project, Unary, Binary) - Unary operations (SELECT, PROJECT) work on ONE table, Binary operations (JOIN, UNION, etc.) work on TWO tables.
keyPoints: Unary operations, like SELECT (σ) and PROJECT (π), are performed on a single relation or table.
Binary operations, like UNION (∪), SET DIFFERENCE (-), CARTESIAN PRODUCT (×), and JOIN (⨝), require two relations or tables to perform.
The result of any relational operation is always another relation, adhering to the closure property of the relational model.
commonMistakes: Confusing PROJECT (π) with SELECT (σ): PROJECT chooses columns, while SELECT chooses rows based on a condition.
Attempting binary operations like UNION on tables that are not union-compatible (i.e., they do not have the same number of columns with matching domains).
Forgetting that a JOIN is essentially a CARTESIAN PRODUCT followed by a SELECT operation.
